Map making workshop  from Wikidata to interactive off-Wiki maps in three steps  Wiki Techstorm, Amsterdam, Sat 23 November 2019 Olaf Janssen, National Library of the Netherlands User:OlafJanssen  //  olaf.janssen@kb.nl  // @ookgezellig
Learning objectives Access to map making resources, SPARQL  examples and Python code snippets to build upon
Tips & tricks welcome!  I’m not a maps / Wikidata / SPARQL  guru….
3 MODULES 1) Basic flat & layered maps 2) Embedded maps in Wikimedia projects 3) Interactive, layered off-Wiki maps  driven by Wikidata
For this Module, you will need • Wikimedia account • Working knowledge of Wikidata… • … and SPARQL • A Wikidata set of items with geo coordinates (P625)….
Time to create  your own P625 dataset

MODULE 2 Embedded maps in Wikimedia projects
For this Module, you will (also) need • User page on Wikipedia, Commons, Wikidata • With Sandbox Nice to have: some knowledge about • OpenStreetMap and • GeoJSON

Copy this whole  GeoJSON to…. Geojson.io

MODULE 3 Interactive, layered off-Wiki maps  driven by Wikidata
For this Module, it’s nice to have  basic experience with • Python • PAWS / Jupyter Notebooks • (Jupyter-Leaflet & Jupyter-widgets)
https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/PAWS Start your PAWS server https://paws.wmflabs.org/paws/hub
You might see this screen… Starting your PAWS server

https://paws.wmflabs.org/paws/user/USERNAME/tree Your PAWS server running .ipynb =  interactive python notebook • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Project_Jupyter#Jupyter_Notebook • https://realpython.com/jupyter-notebook-introduction
https://paws-public.wmflabs.org/paws-public/User:USERNAME Your PAWS public folder (even when server is offline)
• https://jupyter.org/install • https://www.anaconda.com/distribution/ • https://dataiseasy.com/2019/03/how-to-install-anaconda-and-jupyter-notebook PAWS alternative: local Notebook installation
Go to public PAWS folder of User:OlafJanssen Go to this folder https://paws-public.wmflabs.org/paws-public/User:OlafJanssen
In folder ‘WikidataMapMakingWorkshop’ Example notebook  we will use https://paws-public.wmflabs.org/paws- public/User:OlafJanssen/WikidataMapMakingWorkshop/WikidataMapMakingWorkshop.ipynb
This notebook in raw format (json) https://paws-public.wmflabs.org/paws- public/User:OlafJanssen/WikidataMapMakingWorkshop/WikidataMapMakingWorkshop.ipynb ?format=raw
Save this raw file to your PC
… and upload it to your PAWS server https://paws.wmflabs.org/paws/user/USERNAME/tree
… and upload it to your PAWS server

Notebook on your own PAWS server to play with https://paws.wmflabs.org/paws/user/USERNAME/notebooks/WikidataMapMakingWorkshop.ipynb
